feed.title: SAP Community - Data and Analytics
feed.subtitle: Data and Analytics Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Unable to Create Custom Analytical Query in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Good afternoon,

I’m reaching out to ask for some guidance regarding an issue I’m experiencing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

I’m trying to create a new Custom Analytical Query using the Custom Analytical Queries app in the test environment, as I would like to start exploring and working with this functionality. However, when I access the app, the “Create” button is disabled, and I’m unable to proceed.

I have already reviewed the business roles and catalog permissions assigned to my user, and they appear to include the necessary authorizations to create analytical queries.

These are my business roles

These are permissions for business rol 1

These are permissions for business rol 2

I’ve searched through available documentation and tried different approaches, but I haven’t been able to identify the root cause.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or can provide guidance on what additional configuration, role, or restriction I should verify?

Any help would be greatly appreciated.

Kind regards,
Octavio

entry.title: Dynamic Closing Month in SAC (BW Import Model)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m working with a BW Query-based Import Model in SAP Analytics Cloud and I’m facing a design challenge related to time handling.

Business Requirement

We have two different visualization needs within the same story:

  1. Numeric point charts (KPI tiles)
    → Should always display values for a selected “Reporting Month” (Closing Month).

  2. Bar charts (Trend analysis)
    → Should display performance for the last 13 months relative to the selected Closing Month
    (i.e., Closing Month - 12 months up to Closing Month).

Important Constraint

The “Closing Month” is not always Current Month - 1.

Due to operational timing (e.g., delayed invoicing), the closing month is effectively the latest available month in the dataset, which may vary over time.

Additionally:

  • Users must be able to override the Closing Month at runtime
    (e.g., instead of December 2025, they may select November 2025).

  • This selection should:

    • Update KPI tiles to reflect the selected month.

    • Dynamically adjust the bar chart to show the rolling 13-month window relative to that selection (e.g., Nov 2024 – Nov 2025).

Current Approach

Currently, I am:

  • Using a Story Variable for Reporting Month.

  • Programmatically setting filters for widgets based on that variable via scripting.

This works functionally, but:

  • It feels performance-intensive.

  • It introduces additional story scripting complexity.

Question

Within the constraints of a BW Import Model, what would be the recommended architectural approach to:

  • Support a dynamic “Closing Month” selection,

  • Drive both single-month KPIs and rolling 13-month trend charts,

  • Minimize scripting and optimize performance?

Any best practices or design recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

Onur

entry.title: Issues with CDS based Delta Extraction in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello,

I have got some warning and failed messages during Delta load execution.

  1. Error Occurred During API execution Activity
  2. Subscription Interface error
  3. Error while processing subscription of CDS view
  4. Error while reading reconstruction metadata for CDS view xxxxx
  5. CDS view xxxxx is inconsistent
  6. Definition of view is too complex for automatic CDC [warning]

I have not yet released the C1 contract.
If I proceed with the release, will it resolve the issues mentioned above?
Or are there additional changes required from my side?

Also I am pasting the annotations which I have used

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface View for zzxxxxx'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@analytics.dataExtraction.enabled: true
@analytics.dataExtraction.delta.changeDataCapture.automatic: true

entry.title: Passing dynamic values to Input parameters for CDS view for Data extraction from Datashere
entry.content:
Hello All,

We are looking for a solution to pass the dynamic values to the Input Parameters of the CDS view C_InvtryTurnoverAnalysis for the data extraction to Datasphere. We are using this SAP-provided CDS view C_InvtryTurnoverAnalysis in a custom CDS view and passing the static values for the dates currently to extract the Inventory Turnover every month. We did try passing the Date Functions to the Input parameters and getting the syntax errors. SAP Datasphere also has not provided the feature yet to extract the data from CDS views that have Input Parameters.

Below is the Date functions used to pass the dynamic previous month value to the Input parameter in the Custom CDS view which is using C_InvtryTurnoverAnalysis and throwing the syntax error.

P_StartDate : cast(
concat(
substring(
cast( add_months( $session.system_date, -1 ) as abap.char(8) ),
1,
6
),
'01'
)
as abap.dats
)

Following is the date functions tried to pass to the input parameters and getting the syntax errors.

P_StartDate : cast( concat( substring( cast( DATS_ADD_MONTHS( $session.system_date, -1) as abap.char(8) ), 1, 6 ), '01') as abap.dats ),

P_EndDate : DATS_ADD_DAYS(DATS_ADD_MONTHS(cast( concat( substring( cast( DATS_ADD_MONTHS( $session.system_date, -1 ) as abap.char(8) ), 1, 6 ), '01') as abap.dats ),1),-1)

Appreciate if you have come across the similar scenario and will help if any recommendations.

SAP Datasphere ABAP Development SAP BTP ABAP environment

entry.title: Field Visibility Between Story Reports and Canvas Reports in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

I’m observing an inconsistency in SuccessFactors reporting behavior related to the Position object.
Fields that are disabled at the Position object level do not appear in Canvas Reports, which is the expected behavior. However, the same disabled fields continue to be visible in Story Reports.

Is this expected system behavior for Story Reports, or is there an additional configuration required to control field visibility consistently across both reporting types?

Regards,

Ravi

entry.title: Datashpere Migration to S4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I need your guidance regarding the SAP Datasphere migration to S/4HANA.

Previously, my datasphere was connected to ECC. Now, I want to migrate the  to S/4HANA. I have used multiple graphical views and created reporting-layer objects, which are also consumed in SAC.

In ECC, I was using remote tables and integrating them with local tables through data flows. However, in S/4HANA, all master data structures have changed (such as GL, Cost Center, Profit Center, Company Code, Buis area etc.).

Could you please suggest the best approach to handle these changes in the reporting layer?

Where exactly changes required?

Remote table also available in S4HANA.

Note: I need to retain ECC (PY) historical data along with current data from S/4HANA.

@jeet_kapase @cardoso  @N1kh1l 

Thanks in Advance

entry.title: SAC: Data not refreshed by refresh button/auto refresh - No "Live Data" possible?
entry.content:
Dear all,

 

We are facing the following problem:
Data in SAC dashboard are not refreshed when using the standard refresh button or scripts like Application.refreshData().
We need to reopen the browser page in order to get the latest set of data. 

Setup: 
Dashboard -> CDS VM -> S/4HANA Tables

Caching is already disabled in RSRT: 3384063 - Refresh button from SAP Analytics Cloud Story does not update the data for BW live data connection - SAP for Me

May I ask you for any ideas how real "Live Data" could be achieved? 
In the final solution we would like to use the auto refresh function in SAC with an interval of a few minutes.

Thank you

entry.title: SAP S4Hana Public Cloud - Custom Analytical Queries and Services, Default to Previous Month?
entry.content: In the old App "Custom Analytical Queries", I am able to set a date filter with a Default Value of "relative: Previous Month". How do I do this in the new "Custom Analytical Queries and Services" App?

entry.title: Unable to setup multiple file export from XDL, need help with the steps pls
entry.content:
I am trying to export multiple files from a single table on the basis of a grouping column in the table but even after mentioning the group column in the XDL job it throws error saying "grouping column doesnt exist".

Could anyone pls help if anyone has ever explored it?

 

Thanks and Regards,

Sandy

entry.title: Can we please use the 'old' Custom Analytical Queries App to create new Queries again?
entry.content:
One problem I see is that SAP has two apps. One named "Custom Analytical Queries" and another one named "Custom Analytical Queries and Services". Within "Custom Analytical Queries", you can no-longer create new Queries. The issue is that "Custom Analytical Queries and Services" does not have the Aggregation options for some of the elements.

I keep running into situations where the older app would have worked better for me and the new app, keeps missing features. 

Is there a way to turn the old one 'back on' until the new app has more functionality that I miss from the old?

entry.title: Delta enabled replication Options in SAP Datasphere with the focus on using SAP extractors 2LIS
entry.content:
Hi,

Could someone please help me with an issue? I am extracting data from an S/4 system using 2LIS extractors. I have completed all prerequisites, including enabling the primary keys. However, when I try to run the delta-enabled replication, I am getting an error.

Could anyone please guide me on how to resolve this and successfully achieve the required data load?

please find the error attached

entry.title: Missing Association After Data Product Deployment in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

A Sales table is modeled as a fact view with an association to a Product dimension (modeled as a separate graphical view). Both views are exposed for consumption. A data product is created on top of these two artifacts - Sales and Product and deployed. And the data product is installed in the target space while both views are available in the target space, the association is missing. Is this by design.

Could you help clarify why this happens. Thanks!
--
KRPK

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! Aspiring Data Analyst from India
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

My name is Mohammad Zishan Noor, and I am a 3rd-year B.Tech CSE student from India.

I am interested in Data Analytics, SAP Analytics Cloud, Power BI, Python, SQL, and AI. Currently, I am learning SAP technologies and preparing to improve my analytics and cloud skills.

I have worked on projects like:
• SMS Spam Classifier using Machine Learning
• Patient Demographics Analysis Dashboard

I joined the SAP Community to learn from experts, connect with professionals, and grow my career in Data Analytics and SAP technologies.

Looking forward to learning from this amazing community.

Thank you!

entry.title: Guidance Needed: LMS → Snowflake Data Ingestion & Identifying Correct API Endpoints
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We are currently working on a project to ingest SAP SuccessFactors LMS data into Snowflake for reporting and analytics. We’ve reviewed the available documentation, including the Account Data report reference here:

  1.  https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/lm/3354618614.html
  2. https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-learning/sap-successfactors-learning-odata-apis/background-knowledge-for-sap-successfactors-learning-web-services
  3. https://me.sap.com/notes

    At this stage, we are able to retrieve the Student table, but we are struggling to identify the correct API endpoints for additional user‑related fields. Many attributes we need (e.g., extended user profile fields, account‑level metadata, etc.) do not appear in the Student table or in the standard PRD/BIRT report definitions.

    Our questions are:

    1. Which LMS API endpoints expose full user information beyond the Student table?

    2. Is there a recommended approach or best practice for extracting complete user data for external ingestion (e.g., Snowflake, data lake, etc.)?

    3. Has anyone successfully implemented an LMS → Snowflake or similar pipeline and can share which endpoints or reports were required?

    4. Are there hidden or less obvious endpoints for user attributes that are not documented in the standard help pages?

    Any guidance, examples, or direction to the correct documentation would be greatly appreciated.

    Thank you!

entry.title: Unable to Create "Custom Analytical Queries and Services" in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi Expert, 

I would like to add a custom query in my starter system, and i can access to this app "Custom Analytical Queries and Services", but unable to do any movement in this app.

I have checked my authorization which Z_BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST that based on template role "BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST", and all "unrestricted". But I still cannot edit or create in that query app.

And also check IAM app is active.

Is there any authorization role or catelog I am lacking of?

 

Tina

entry.title: S4Hana Public Cloud Manage Date Functions App - Can we add Fiscal Year to the Categories?
entry.content: In the App - Manage Date Functions, we would like to expand the options for customization within this app. Currently, you can set date filters for time frames such as Week, Month, Year and Quarter. Is it possible to create a category for Fiscal Year within this framework?

entry.title: Project Cost Line Items – Loss of Cumulative and Commitment-Based Measures in Recent Releases
entry.content:
Project Cost Line Items – Loss of Cumulative and Commitment-Based Measures in Recent Releases

I would like to raise a concern regarding the evolution of the Project Cost Line Items application.

In older S/4HANA releases, the application F2538 – Project Cost Line Items provided several analytical measures that were extremely useful for project controlling and cost analysis.

Reference:

  • SAP Help document 

  • SAP Fiori App Library: F2538

As shown in SAP documentation and screenshots from earlier releases, users could analyze project costs using measures such as:

  • Cumulative Actual Cost

  • Cumulative Planned Cost

  • Cumulative Actual Cost + Commitments

  • Commitment values originating from open Purchase Requisitions and Purchase Orders

These measures enabled the creation of trend analyses and S-curves, allowing project managers to compare:

  • Planned vs. Actual evolution over time

  • Actual + Commitment evolution over time

  • Cost performance by fiscal period and fiscal year using cumulative values

The chart below illustrates the type of cumulative analysis that was possible in previous releases, where values evolved period after period rather than being displayed as isolated figures.

 

Since S/4HANA 2023, the application has been replaced by F6992 – Project Costs – Line Items.

Reference:

  • SAP Help document 

  • SAP Fiori App Library: F6992

However, the newer application appears to have lost several important analytical capabilities.

The cumulative measures mentioned above are no longer available, and commitment-related measures seem to have been removed as well. As a result:

  • Users can only analyze values for individual periods.

  • Cumulative cost evolution cannot be displayed.

  • Actual Cost + Commitments analysis is no longer possible.

  • Commitment visibility (open Purchase Requisitions and Purchase Orders) is missing.

  • Building project cost S-curves directly from the standard application is no longer feasible.

From a project controlling perspective, this feels like a regression in reporting capabilities rather than an enhancement.

Has anyone found an alternative standard application that provides the same cumulative and commitment-based analysis?

Is there any roadmap item, SAP Note, or Customer Influence request addressing the reintroduction of these measures in F6992?

I would be interested in understanding how other customers and consultants are handling this limitation in S/4HANA 2023 and 2025.

Regards
Marcio Blos

entry.title: Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise Webinar Series - two sessions this week on 14th and 16th July
entry.content:
We have Two sessions coming up this week in the Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise webinar series, both directly relevant for anyone making architectural decisions around AI.
 
Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise is a 13-part webinar series designed by architects for architects. Each session unpacks a different dimension of the Autonomous Enterprise: from the Business AI Platform and agent development to data foundations, governance, and the Context System. It is a community learning journey where SAP architects, product leaders, and customers come together to work through the What, why, and How of building for an agentic world. All sessions are complimentary and available on-demand.
 
This week We have two sessions!
 
Session 7 - Monday 14 July | 17:00 CET / 11:00 ET / 08:00 PT An Architect's Guide to the SAP Family of Models With Markus Kohler, Simon Knehr , and Ashok Veilumuthu
SAP is the only company that has trained AI models on the full depth of its institutional knowledge — decades of business process expertise, application code, financial outcomes, and industry best practices. This session explores what the SAP Family of Models is, how business context delivers 3.5x higher prediction quality than generic LLMs, and what it means for architects evaluating AI model strategies. If you are deciding between generic foundation models and purpose-built enterprise models — or trying to explain the difference to your leadership — this is the session.
 
Session 8 - Wednesday 16 July | 14:00 CET / 08:00 ET / 05:00 PT -  The AI North Star Architecture and AI Golden Path: From Reference to Reality
 
Every architect asks: what does the target architecture look like, and how do I get there? This session answers both - covering the AI-Native North Star Architecture (how AI, data, applications, and agents converge as a unified system), the AI Golden Path (a guided decision framework from idea to production) and the Architecture Center in General. Includes live demonstrations and worked examples showing how reference patterns translate into real implementation decisions. If you have been looking at the published assets on the SAP Architecture Center and wondering how to apply them, this is where it comes together.
Register once, attend any session: https://events.sap.com/eaa-autent-web/en_us/home.html

You can download and import the updated calendar file to ensure all remaining sessions are added to your calendar.

 Slides and recordings from previous session here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/slides-amp-recordings-from-the-13-part-architecting-the-autonomous/ba-p/14432238#M191674

Feel free to share this with to anyone in your architecture, AI, or technology leadership teams who would benefit.

entry.title: S/4HANA Outbound Flows - How the API Policy apply ?
entry.content:
My customer wants to evaluate an option (without BDC) to extract the data for an Analytical usecase.

S/4HANA PCE > Mulesoft > Snowflake

Is that usecase supported or restricted ? Does the API Policy apply ? 

What fees / additional cost should we expect ?

entry.title: SAC/DSP to connect with SAP ECC (underlying oracle database)SAP 752 version to get live data ?
entry.content:
Hi Team

We have a requirement where client want to get in SAP SAC/DSP system with live data connections from SAP ECC underlying database is oracle/SAP 752 version .. .

possible solution we have thinking :-

1)if we create a CDS view in SAP ECC system and connect through SAP ERP connections and Can it  possible to get the CDS view as remote table in our SAC side ?
2) if we can create an Odata connection from SAC to SAP ECC , Can we get live data from the CDS views to SAC ?

Could you please suggest possible solution and which approach will  work and limitations here ? to achieve it, is there any configuration setting we need to do in SAP ECC side  , if yes please suggest. 

 can datasphere help to achieve the live data connections from ECC , please suggest if any?   

Regards

Sumit
